artifact: DirectLLM RCA: Weekly-statement accounts should first be compared with other frequencies on 1997 finished-loan default rate. Then test whether the weekly segment is overrepresented in lower-salary districts, larger loan amount quartiles, or longer durations. Treat these as correlational drivers; a convincing explanation requires both segment concentration and elevated within-segment default rates.
sql: WITH cohort AS (SELECT a.frequency, l.status, l.amount, l.duration, d.A11 AS avg_salary, NTILE(4) OVER (ORDER BY d.A11) AS salary_quartile, NTILE(4) OVER (ORDER BY l.amount) AS amount_quartile FROM loan l JOIN account a ON a.account_id=l.account_id JOIN district d ON d.district_id=a.district_id WHERE EXTRACT(YEAR FROM CAST(l.date AS DATE))=1997 AND l.status IN ('A','B')) SELECT frequency, salary_quartile, amount_quartile, CASE WHEN duration=12 THEN 'short_12' WHEN duration IN (24,36) THEN 'medium_24_36' ELSE 'long_48_60' END AS duration_bucket, COUNT(*) AS finished_loans, SUM(CASE WHEN status='B' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS defaults, SUM(CASE WHEN status='B' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)::DECIMAL/NULLIF(COUNT(*),0) AS default_rate, AVG(amount) AS avg_loan_amount, AVG(duration) AS avg_duration, AVG(avg_salary) AS avg_district_salary FROM cohort GROUP BY frequency,salary_quartile,amount_quartile,duration_bucket ORDER BY frequency, default_rate DESC;

charts.title: 1997 Default Rate by Frequency and Driver
